title: Statement of profit or loss
summary: 9609 A Level — income statement layout, gross and operating profit, and performance interpretation.
learningObjectives: Interpret a statement of profit or loss.
Distinguish gross profit, operating profit, and profit for the year.
Explain the role of the income statement in analysis.
Calculate missing figures from case data.

simpleExplanation.title: The income story
simpleExplanation.summary: The statement of profit or loss (income statement) shows revenue, costs, and profit over a period — revealing how well the business traded.
simpleExplanation.analogy: Think of it as a film of the year, not a photograph. The balance sheet is a snapshot on one day; the profit or loss statement plays through every sale and expense from January to December.
steps: Revenue minus cost of sales = gross profit.
Finance costs and tax to profit for the year.

flashcards.front: What does the statement of profit or loss show?
flashcards.back: Income and expenses over a period, leading to profit or loss for the year.
Gross profit minus operating expenses (overheads such as admin, marketing, wages not in COGS).
Operating profit ± finance items − tax (final bottom line for shareholders).
Direct costs of producing goods sold: materials, direct labour, production overheads tied to output.
Identifies growth, margin pressure, cost control, and trends for ratio analysis (10.2).
Revenue recognised when earned (accruals); may differ from cash if credit sales used.
Profitability ratios (GPM, OPM, ROCE) use figures from this statement.

sections.content: The **statement of profit or loss** (10.1.1) opens **A Level Topic 10 Accounts**. Paper 3/4 questions ask you to **complete layouts**, **calculate missing lines**, and **interpret trends**. Learn standard **Cambridge labels** — examiners deduct marks for wrong terminology.

Typical structure (top to bottom):

**Revenue** (sales)
− **Cost of sales**
= **Gross profit**
− **Operating expenses** (distribution, admin, etc.)
= **Operating profit**
± **Finance costs / income**
− **Tax**
= **Profit for the year**

items: **Gross profit** measures core trading margin before overheads.
**Operating profit** shows performance from normal operations.
**Profit for the year** is what remains for retention or dividends after finance and tax.

sections.question:
Revenue $820 000; cost of sales $492 000; operating expenses $218 000; finance cost $12 000; tax $24 000.

Calculate gross profit, operating profit, and profit for the year.

sections.content: Show **each line** in calculations even if the question only asks for one figure — method marks are common. Use $ and thousands consistently with the case.

sections.label: Mark a profit or loss question
